identity.name: FTP Cleartext Egress + Credential Exposure — Outbound STOR Exfiltration (T1071 / T1048.003)
identity.summary: A non-admin endpoint opens an outbound FTP control session to a host outside the corporate range, authenticates with cleartext USER/PASS commands, and pushes data with STOR. The CortexSim ftp_egress EAL plugin reproduces the full cleartext FTP shape so NGFW App-ID identifies the session as ftp and XSIAM Network Security Analytics flags both the cleartext credential exposure and the outbound fil…
identity.description: FTP remains a favored exfiltration channel precisely because it is unencrypted, ubiquitous on internal file-transfer appliances, and frequently allowed outbound through legacy firewall rules. The control channel carries credentials in the clear (USER <name> / PASS <secret>) and the STOR command moves file payloads out of the environment. The wire signature Cortex detects is threefold: (1) NGFW App-ID resolving an outbound session to the ftp application from a non-admin source toward a destination outside the corporate CIDR; (2) cleartext credential material observed in the FTP command stream (USER + PASS), which NGFW EAL surfaces as an IOC; and (3) an outbound file transfer via the STOR command carrying a data payload, behaviourally anomalous from a workstation. This card detects all three from panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story, and a correlation rule fuses the App-ID, the cleartext-credential IOC, and the STOR-upload BIOC into a single data-exfiltration incident so the SOC sees one story rather than three disconnected traffic events.

execution.framework_reference_id: T1048.003 — CortexSim ftp_egress EAL plugin (outbound FTP USER/PASS + STOR to lab sinkhole)

prerequisites.description: Lab environment matching scenario SIM-NDR-006 requirements (NGFW logging outbound traffic from the lab subnet to XSIAM, FTP sinkhole on 10.50.0.42:21).
NGFW forwards outbound traffic logs into XSIAM (panw_ngfw_traffic_raw / network_story carries app, src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port).

payload.code:
# Live cleartext FTP session: USER/PASS auth + STOR data egress to lab sinkhole.
cat > /tmp/ftp-egress-live.yml <<'YML'
campaign_id: CMP-NDR-006
name: "NDR validation — FTP cleartext credential + STOR egress"
authorized_by: "domain-consultant@paloaltonetworks.com"
simulation_authorized: true
target_allowlist:
  - 10.50.0.0/24
dry_run: false
tags: [ndr, ftp, eal, cleartext, exfiltration]
steps:
  - step_id: step-01
    plugin: ftp_egress
    params:
      target_host: "10.50.0.42"
      target_port: 21
      username: "cortexsim"
      password: "cortexsim-lab"
      iterations: 3
      sleep_seconds: 8
      send_stor: true
      stor_bytes: 4096
YML
python3 -m scripts.eal_simulator.cli run /tmp/ftp-egress-live.yml --live

expected_artifacts.description: Outbound TCP session on port 21 identified by NGFW App-ID as ftp toward a non-corporate destination.
Cleartext FTP credentials (USER + PASS commands) observed in the control channel.
Outbound file transfer initiated with the FTP STOR command carrying a data payload.

iocs.context: NGFW EAL — cleartext FTP credentials observed (USER + PASS commands in session). Cleartext USER and PASS command strings present in the outbound FTP control channel.

biocs.name: NGFW EAL outbound FTP STOR file transfer with data payload
biocs.description: NGFW EAL — outbound file transfer via FTP STOR command + 4 KB data payload
biocs.logic:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter app = "ftp" or dst_port = 21
| filter ftp_command = "STOR" or action_total_upload > 0
| filter dst_ip != null and not incidr(dst_ip, "10.0.0.0/8")
| comp count() as stor_sessions, sum(action_total_upload) as bytes_out by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port
| filter bytes_out > 0
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, stor_sessions, bytes_out

xql_queries.name: Pre-flight FTP sinkhole reachable on port 21
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter dst_port = 21
| filter dst_ip = "10.50.0.42"
| comp count() as control_sessions by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, control_sessions

xql_queries.name: NGFW App-ID FTP outbound session from non-admin endpoint
xql_queries.query:
dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app = "ftp" or dst_port = 21
| filter not incidr(dst_ip, "10.0.0.0/8") or dst_ip = "10.50.0.42"
| comp count() as ftp_sessions by src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app
| filter ftp_sessions >= 1
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, dst_port, app, ftp_sessions

xql_queries.name: XSIAM correlation FTP cleartext creds plus STOR egress stitched
xql_queries.query:
preset = network_story
| filter _time > to_timestamp(current_time() - 1800)
| filter app = "ftp" or dst_port = 21
| comp count() as ftp_sessions, sum(action_total_upload) as bytes_out, values(ftp_command) as commands by src_ip, dst_ip
| filter bytes_out > 0
| fields src_ip, dst_ip, ftp_sessions, bytes_out, commands

correlation_rules.name: FTP Cleartext Egress — Credential Exposure Plus STOR Upload
correlation_rules.logic: IOC(ftp-cleartext-credentials-user-pass) OR BIOC(NGFW EAL outbound FTP STOR file transfer with data payload) OR XQL(NGFW App-ID FTP outbound session from non-admin endpoint)

analytics_modules: Cortex XSIAM Network Security Analytics — Cleartext Protocol Exfiltration
Cortex XSIAM Analytics — Outbound FTP Data Transfer to Non-Corporate Destination

products.evidence_query: dataset = panw_ngfw_traffic_raw | filter app = "ftp" | comp sum(action_total_upload) as bytes_out by src_ip, dst_ip
preset = network_story | filter app = "ftp" | comp sum(action_total_upload) as bytes_out by src_ip, dst_ip | filter bytes_out > 0
Playbook: block the outbound FTP destination, rotate the exposed credentials, quarantine the source host, open a data-exfiltration DFIR ticket.

use_cases.name: Cleartext FTP Exfiltration Detection via NGFW EAL
use_cases.description: Validate that NGFW App-ID + XSIAM Network Security Analytics detect outbound FTP with cleartext credentials and STOR data egress from a non-admin endpoint.

test_cases.objective: Outbound FTP App-ID and cleartext credentials are detected from NGFW telemetry.
preconditions: NGFW logging outbound traffic from the lab subnet to XSIAM.
steps: Run SIM-NDR-006 step-02 (live ftp_egress with USER/PASS + STOR).
Inspect Network Security Analytics / network_story for the source IP.
success_criteria: XQL `NGFW App-ID FTP outbound session from non-admin endpoint` returns >= 1 row.
IOC `NGFW EAL cleartext FTP credentials USER PASS in session` matches the session.

test_cases.objective: STOR upload and credential exposure stitch into a single exfiltration incident.
success_criteria: BIOC `NGFW EAL outbound FTP STOR file transfer with data payload` returns >= 1 row with bytes_out > 0.
Correlation `CR-NDR-0005` raises a single data-exfiltration incident for the source.

preventive_controls: Block cleartext FTP egress at the NGFW; require SFTP/FTPS for any sanctioned file transfer.
Apply default-deny outbound policy and allow file-transfer applications only from approved transfer hosts.
Enforce DLP on outbound file-transfer protocols to detect and block sensitive data egress.
detection_engineering: Alert on ftp App-ID from non-admin endpoints toward non-corporate destinations.
Surface cleartext USER/PASS credential exposure observed in FTP control channels.
Correlate STOR uploads with the credential exposure and App-ID into one exfiltration incident.

references.title: Unit42 - Cleartext Protocol Abuse for Exfiltration
MITRE ATT&CK T1048.003 — Exfiltration Over Unencrypted Non-C2 Protocol
